channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 191724
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
1100 AM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Elida, located well west of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula, and on Tropical Depression Six-E, located
several hundred miles southwest of southwestern Mexico.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A broad area of low pressure is forecast to develop well south of
Mexico during the latter part of the week. Environmental
conditions appear conducive for development of the system
thereafter, and a tropical depression could form while it moves
westward to west-northwestward across the eastern and central
portions of the eastern Pacific.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...medium...40 percent.

South-Southwest of Hawaii:
Disorganized showers and thunderstorms located several hundred miles
south-southwest of the Hawaiian Islands are associated with
a trough of low pressure. Environmental conditions appear
conducive for some gradual development of this system as it moves
westward and away from the Hawaiian islands during the next
several days.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...low...20 percent.

&&

Public Advisories on Tropical Depression Six-E are issued under
WMO header WTPZ31 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCPEP1.
Forecast/Advisories on Tropical Depression Six-E are issued under
WMO header WTPZ21 KNHC and under AWIPS header MIATCMEP1.

$$
Forecaster Katz/Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...DEPRESSION STRENGTHENS INTO A TROPICAL STORM... As of 2:00 PM MST Sun Jul 19 the center of Fausto was located near 12.4, -112.7 with movement WNW at 13 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1005 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 40 mph.

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Public Advisory Number 4
item.description:
Issued at 200 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 192036
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Fausto Advisory Number   4
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
200 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026
 
...DEPRESSION STRENGTHENS INTO A TROPICAL STORM...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 200 PM MST...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...12.4N 112.7W
ABOUT 750 MI...1205 KM SSW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...40 MPH...65 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 285 DEGREES AT 13 MPH...20 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1005 MB...29.68 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 200 PM MST (2100 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Fausto was
located near latitude 12.4 North, longitude 112.7 West.  Fausto is
moving toward the west-northwest near 13 mph (20 km/h) and a 
west-northwestward or northwestward motion at a slightly slower 
forward speed is expected over the next couple of days.
 
Maximum sustained winds have increased to near 40 mph (65 km/h) 
with higher gusts.  Steady to rapid strengthening is forecast during 
the next couple of days, and Fausto is forecast to become a 
hurricane by Monday night.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 80 miles (130 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1005 mb (29.68 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
None.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 800 PM MST.
 
$$
Forecaster Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 4
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026 
000
WTPZ21 KNHC 192036
TCMEP1
 
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER   4
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 12.4N 112.7W AT 19/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 285 DEGREES AT  11 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1005 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  35 KT WITH GUSTS TO  45 KT.
34 KT.......  0NE   0SE   0SW  70NW.
4 M SEAS.... 30NE   0SE   0SW  30NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 12.4N 112.7W AT 19/2100Z
AT 19/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 12.3N 112.2W
 
FORECAST VALID 20/0600Z 13.1N 114.1W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT... 70NE  30SE   0SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 20/1800Z 14.2N 115.8W
MAX WIND  55 KT...GUSTS  65 KT.
50 KT... 30NE  20SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT... 80NE  50SE  40SW  80NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0600Z 15.4N 117.3W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
64 KT... 20NE  15SE   0SW  15NW.
50 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  30NW.
34 KT...100NE 100SE  60SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1800Z 16.3N 118.7W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 20NE  15SE  15SW  20NW.
50 KT... 50NE  40SE  30SW  50NW.
34 KT...110NE 100SE  70SW 110NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0600Z 16.7N 120.1W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  30SE  20SW  30NW.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...130NE 130SE  90SW 110NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 17.0N 121.8W
MAX WIND  90 KT...GUSTS 110 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  50SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 130SE 110SW 120NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 23/1800Z 17.6N 126.0W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
50 KT... 80NE  60SE  50SW  60NW.
34 KT...160NE 140SE 110SW 150NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 24/1800Z 18.5N 131.3W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  50SW  70NW.
34 KT...160NE 140SE 110SW 160NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 12.4N 112.7W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER PASCH
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 4
item.description:
Issued at 200 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ41 KNHC 192036
TCDEP1
 
Tropical Storm Fausto Discussion Number   4
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
200 PM MST Sun Jul 19 2026
 
The system is becoming more consolidated in appearance, with less 
southwest to northeast elongation than seen earlier today.  A 
recent ASCAT pass over the system showed a believable 32-kt surface 
wind vector over the northwest quadrant, and TAFB gave a subjective 
Dvorak T-number of 2.5.  This is in agreement with objective AIDT 
intensity estimates of 35 kt from UW-CIMSS.  Based on this 
information, the system is being upgraded to Tropical Storm Fausto 
on this advisory.  The storm has fairly well-defined upper-level 
outflow over its southern semicircle.

There is still some uncertainty in the center position, but the 
best estimate of initial motion is 285/11 kt.  A mid-tropospheric 
ridge lies to the north of Fausto with a weakness in the ridge, 
associated with departing Tropical Storm Elida, to the northwest.  
The ridge should gradually build back westward in Elida's 
wake.  However, there will probably be just enough remaining 
weakness in the ridge to induce a slightly more northwestward 
motion of Fausto during the next 48-72 hours.  The NHC track 
forecast is a blend of the Google DeepMind and corrected dynamical 
model consensus guidance.

The environment ahead of Fausto looks quite conducive for steady to 
rapid intensification, with low vertical wind shear and a very 
moist low- to mid-level air mass.  The SHIPS Rapid Intensification 
(RI) Index shows a significantly higher than normal likelihood of 
RI, and the official intensity forecast explicitly shows RI 
occurring in 24 to 48 hours.  This rapid strengthening could 
occur sooner depending on how soon Fausto develops a 
well-defined inner core.

FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  19/2100Z 12.4N 112.7W   35 KT  40 MPH
 12H  20/0600Z 13.1N 114.1W   45 KT  50 MPH
 24H  20/1800Z 14.2N 115.8W   55 KT  65 MPH
 36H  21/0600Z 15.4N 117.3W   70 KT  80 MPH
 48H  21/1800Z 16.3N 118.7W   85 KT 100 MPH
 60H  22/0600Z 16.7N 120.1W   95 KT 110 MPH
 72H  22/1800Z 17.0N 121.8W   90 KT 105 MPH
 96H  23/1800Z 17.6N 126.0W   85 KT 100 MPH
120H  24/1800Z 18.5N 131.3W   75 KT  85 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 4
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 192036
PWSEP1
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER   4           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 2100Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 12.4 NORTH...LONGITUDE 112.7 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 35 KTS...40 MPH...65 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z SUN AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM

PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             06Z MON 18Z MON 06Z TUE 18Z TUE 18Z WED 18Z THU 18Z FRI

- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 115W       34  X  30(30)   8(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)   X(38)
15N 115W       50  X   6( 6)   3( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)   X( 9)

15N 120W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   6( 6)  14(20)   X(20)   X(20)
15N 120W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   4( 5)   X( 5)   X( 5)
15N 120W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)   X( 2)
 
20N 120W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)   X( 5)   1( 6)
 
15N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  12(13)   1(14)
15N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)   X( 3)
15N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)
 
20N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  26(27)   2(29)
20N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   5( 5)   1( 6)
20N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   1( 3)

20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   9( 9)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  17(17)

$$

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Elida (EP5/EP052026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...ELIDA EXPECTED TO WEAKEN TO A REMNANT LOW WITHIN A COUPLE OF DAYS... As of 2:00 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 the center of Elida was located near 23.2, -125.9 with movement NNW at 10 mph. The minimum central pressure was 996 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 60 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Elida
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...ELIDA EXPECTED TO WEAKEN TO A REMNANT LOW WITHIN A COUPLE OF DAYS...

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Public Advisory Number 21
item.description:
Issued at 200 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ35 KNHC 192039
TCPEP5
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Elida Advisory Number  21
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
200 PM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026
 
...ELIDA EXPECTED TO WEAKEN TO A REMNANT LOW WITHIN A COUPLE OF
DAYS...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 200 PM PDT...2100 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...23.2N 125.9W
ABOUT 1015 MI...1635 KM W OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...60 MPH...95 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NNW OR 340 DEGREES AT 10 MPH...17 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...996 MB...29.42 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 200 PM PDT (2100 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Elida was
located near latitude 23.2 North, longitude 125.9 West. Elida is
moving toward the north-northwest near 10 mph (17 km/h). The storm
is expected to increase in forward speed as it turns more northward 
over the next couple of days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 60 mph (95 km/h) with higher gusts.
Weakening is expected during the next couple of days, and the 
cyclone is likely to dissipate by late Wednesday.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 185 miles (295 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 996 mb (29.42 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Elida will affect portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and southern California
through early next week. These swells will likely result in
life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please consult
products from your local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 800 PM PDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Katz/Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Forecast Advisory Number 21
item.description:
Issued at 2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026 
275 
WTPZ25 KNHC 192038
TCMEP5

NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026
2100 UTC SUN JUL 19 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 23.2N 125.9W AT 19/2100Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  25 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTH-NORTHWEST OR 340 DEGREES AT   9 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  996 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  50 KT WITH GUSTS TO  60 KT.
50 KT....... 50NE   0SE   0SW  50NW.
34 KT.......160NE 120SE  70SW 160NW.
4 M SEAS....240NE 180SE 300SW 270NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 23.2N 125.9W AT 19/2100Z
AT 19/1800Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 22.6N 125.7W
 
FORECAST VALID 20/0600Z 24.8N 126.6W
MAX WIND  45 KT...GUSTS  55 KT.
34 KT...140NE  90SE  30SW 130NW.

34 KT... 90NE  40SE   0SW  70NW.

MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 40NE  20SE   0SW  30NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1800Z 32.7N 127.5W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0600Z 34.9N 127.5W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW

FORECAST VALID 22/1800Z 37.1N 127.4W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  20 KT...GUSTS  30 KT.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 23/1800Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 23.2N 125.9W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/0300Z
 
$$
FORECASTER KATZ
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Forecast Discussion Number 20
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026 
000
WTPZ45 KNHC 191440
TCDEP5
 
Tropical Storm Elida Discussion Number  20
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 AM PDT Sun Jul 19 2026

Satellite imagery indicates that Elida has been able to hold on to 
some deep convection in the inner core for the last several hours. 
A 0743 UTC OSCAT scatterometer pass sampled some of the peak 
surface winds around 45 kt. However, the latest subjective Dvorak 
intensity estimate from TAFB remained at T3.5/55 kt. Given the 
current satellite presentation and the other data on hand, the 
intensity has been held at 50 kt.
 
The initial motion is north-northwestward, or 340/8 kt. Elida is
expected to continue turning northward while gradually accelerating
over the next day or two as it becomes increasingly steered by a
deep-layer trough off the California coast. The track guidance 
maintains a fairly good consistency from the last several periods. 
As such only very minor adjustments have been made to the NHC 
forecast track. 

Although the system still has a good presentation on satellite 
images, the cyclone is moving over sea-surface temperatures below 25 
degrees C.  Ocean waters are expected to cool further as the system 
continues to move northward over the next couple days. In addition, 
increasing southwesterly vertical wind shear and drier mid-level 
air should result in a steady weakening trend. Elida is forecast to 
lose its deep convection and become a remnant low by Monday night, 
with the remnant low dissipating by late Wednesday. The NHC 
intensity forecast is essentially unchanged from the previous 
advisory.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  19/1500Z 22.2N 125.3W   50 KT  60 MPH
 12H  20/0000Z 23.7N 126.0W   45 KT  50 MPH
 24H  20/1200Z 26.0N 126.8W   40 KT  45 MPH
 36H  21/0000Z 28.6N 127.4W   35 KT  40 MPH
 48H  21/1200Z 31.5N 127.4W   30 KT  35 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 60H  22/0000Z 34.0N 127.5W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 72H  22/1200Z 36.0N 127.4W   20 KT  25 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 96H  23/1200Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Katz/Pasch
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

LATITUDE 23.2 NORTH...LONGITUDE 125.9 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 50 KTS...60 MPH...95 KM/H.

   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS

   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...

FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        18Z SUN AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.

PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO

$$                                                                  
FORECASTER KATZ
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Graphics
item.description: Wind Speed Probabilities last updated Sun, 19 Jul 2026 15:27:20 GMT
